question_text: A student rubs a polythene rod with a cloth and it becomes negatively charged. What does this tell us about the electrons on the polythene rod?

options.label: A
options.text: Electrons have been removed from the rod

options.label: B
options.text: Electrons have been added to the rod

options.label: C
options.text: Protons have moved onto the rod

explanation: When an object becomes negatively charged, it means it has gained extra electrons. Since polythene becomes negative when rubbed, electrons must have transferred onto it from the cloth.
